title: Building a Crypto Store for Selling Digital Downloads: What Actually Works When You Are in a Restricted Country
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: You made the product. The platform blocked you. Here is how to sell it anyway — to anyone, anywhere, with no gatekeeper in the way.

content.text: Having a great digital product to sell is only half the battle, especially when you are in a country where platforms like PayPal, Stripe, Gumroad, and Payhip are not available or have restrictive requirements, leaving you wondering how to collect payment from buyers, it's a frustrating situation that many creators face, and it's not just about finding an alternative, but about building a reliable and automated system to deliver your digital products to customers, this is where crypto-native infrastructure comes into play, offering a solution that is global, fully automated, and free from the constraints of traditional payment processors
What You Are Actually Comparing

When evaluating options for selling digital products, you are essentially comparing different payment infrastructures, each with its own set of limitations and requirements, traditional platforms like PayPal and Stripe have geographical restrictions and know-your-customer requirements that can be a hurdle for many creators, on the other hand, crypto-native infrastructure offers a more open and accessible solution, allowing creators to sell their products without being tied to a specific payment processor or geography
Where the Traditional Platforms Break Down

Traditional platforms like Gumroad and Payhip often break down for creators in restricted countries, not just because of geographical limitations, but also due to issues with payment verification, delivery automation, and transaction fees, these platforms can be inflexible and often have requirements that are difficult to meet, such as having a bank account in a specific country or providing extensive documentation, this is where crypto-native solutions like Unchained Commerce's Core Shop Engine can provide a more reliable and efficient alternative
What Crypto-Native Infrastructure Does Differently

Crypto-native infrastructure does things differently by providing a decentralized and automated system for selling digital products, with no need for a traditional payment processor, this means that creators can sell their products globally, without being restricted by geographical limitations or know-your-customer requirements, additionally, crypto-native infrastructure often provides instant automated delivery, and real-time analytics, making it a more efficient and reliable solution for creators
The ability to sell digital products globally, without being restricted by geographical limitations or know-your-customer requirements, is a game-changer for creators in restricted countries
The Real Cost Difference

When comparing traditional platforms to crypto-native infrastructure, the cost difference can be significant, traditional platforms often charge transaction fees, and may have additional costs for payment processing and delivery, crypto-native infrastructure, on the other hand, can provide a more cost-effective solution, with lower transaction fees, and no need for expensive payment processing or delivery services, this can make a big difference for creators who are just starting out, or who have limited budgets
Which One to Use and Why

Ultimately, the choice between traditional platforms and crypto-native infrastructure depends on the specific needs of the creator, if you are looking for a global, fully automated, and cost-effective solution, then crypto-native infrastructure may be the way to go, with products like Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle, which includes the Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, creators can have a comprehensive and reliable system for selling digital products, without being tied to traditional payment processors or limited by geographical restrictions
What Creators in Restricted Countries Are Asking

If you're wondering how to sell an ebook without using Gumroad or Payhip, or how to sell online courses without a Stripe account, the answer lies in crypto-native infrastructure, which provides a decentralized and automated system for delivering digital products, with instant payment verification, and real-time analytics, this means that you can focus on creating and selling your products, without worrying about the complexities of payment processing, and with products like Unchained Commerce's Core Shop Engine, and Web3 Wallet Connect, you can have a reliable and efficient system for delivering your digital products to customers

Crypto payment infrastructure provides a level of platform independence that traditional payment processors cannot match, allowing creators to focus on their products, rather than the complexities of payment processing
Related Topics Worth Reading

For creators who sell ebooks, courses, templates, fonts, presets, beats, stock photos, plugins, or software, the ability to sell and deliver digital products automatically is crucial, and with crypto-native infrastructure, this is now possible, regardless of your geographical location, or the type of product you are selling, you can have a global and fully automated system for delivering digital products, and with related topics like selling ebooks with crypto payments, and digital product delivery automation, you can learn more about how to take advantage of this technology
Your Launch Checklist — No Platform Permission Required

Before launching your crypto store, make sure you have a clear plan in place, this includes setting up your Core Shop Engine, and integrating it with Web3 Wallet Connect, and Admin Dashboard, to provide a seamless and automated experience for your customers, you should also test your payment and delivery systems, to ensure that everything is working smoothly, and that you can provide instant automated delivery, and real-time analytics, to your customers, with a comprehensive system like Unchained Commerce's Full Stack Bundle, you can have everything you need to get started, and to succeed in selling digital products
